2:
Flowchart: Maternal Mortality — Nurse Midwife Role
Identify Danger Signs in Pregnancy
↓
Assess: Bleeding / Seizure / Sepsis
↓
Direct Causes
(PPH, Eclampsia, Sepsis)
Indirect Causes
(Anaemia, Heart Disease)
↓
Emergency First Aid & Stabilisation
↓
Timely Referral to Facility
↓
Definitive Management at Hospital
↓
Reduced Maternal Mortality
3:
Flowchart: ANC Schedule (WHO / GoI)
1st Visit — Before 12 Weeks (Booking)
↓
History, Examination, Blood & Urine Tests
↓
2nd Visit — 14–26 Weeks
↓
Review Labs, TT-2, IFA, Anomaly Scan
↓
3rd Visit — 28–32 Weeks
↓
Assess Foetal Growth, BP, Oedema
↓
4th Visit — 36 Weeks Onwards
↓
Presentation, Pelvic Assessment, Birth Plan
↓
Delivery Planning & Institutional Referral
4:
Flowchart: Safe Motherhood Initiative
Pre-pregnancy / Preconception Care
↓
Quality Antenatal Care (4+ Visits)
↓
Skilled Birth Attendant at Delivery
↓
Emergency Obstetric Care Access
↓
Postnatal Care (Mother & Newborn)
↓
Family Planning & Birth Spacing
↓
Safe Motherhood & Healthy Outcome
5:
Flowchart: Reproductive Health Continuum
Preconception Care & Counselling
↓
Antenatal Care (1st–3rd Trimester)
↓
Intranatal Care — Safe Delivery
↓
Postnatal Care (Mother)
Newborn Care (Child)
↓
Family Planning & Well-Woman Care
↓
Optimal Women's & Child Health
6:
Flowchart: Partograph — Use & Interpretation
Begin Partograph in Active Labour (≥4 cm)
↓
Plot: Cervical Dilation, Foetal Descent
↓
Record: FHR, Contractions, Vitals, Liquor
↓
Plot on Alert Line (1 cm/hr expected)
↓
Progress Left of Alert Line — Normal Labour
Reaches Alert Line — Augment / Refer
↓
Crosses Action Line → Immediate Intervention
↓
Safe Delivery / Prevent Obstructed Labour
10:
Flowchart: Skilled Birth Attendant (SBA) Role
Trained SBA Present at Every Birth
↓
Monitor Labour with Partograph
↓
Conduct Clean & Safe Delivery
↓
Active Management of 3rd Stage (AMTSL)
↓
Complication Detected — PPH / Sepsis
Normal — Postnatal Monitoring
↓
First Aid & Emergency Referral if Needed
↓
Reduced Maternal & Neonatal Mortality
11:
Flowchart: Maternal Mortality — Causes & Prevention
Maternal Death (Death During Pregnancy / 42 Days)
↓
Direct Causes
PPH, Eclampsia, Sepsis, Abortion
Indirect Causes
Anaemia, Malaria, Heart Disease
↓
Three Delays Model (Recognition, Reaching, Receiving care)
↓
Prevention: Quality ANC + SBA + EmOC
↓
Role of Nurse: Early Detection & Referral
↓
Sustainable Development Goal — MMR <70/lakh
14:
Flowchart: Menstrual Cycle
Menstruation (Day 1–5)
↓
Follicular Phase (Day 1–13)
FSH → follicle growth → oestrogen rise
↓
Ovulation (Day 14)
LH surge → Graafian follicle ruptures
↓
Luteal Phase (Day 15–28)
Corpus luteum → progesterone
↓
No Fertilization
Corpus luteum degenerates → menstruation
Fertilization occurs
HCG maintains corpus luteum → pregnancy
15:
Flowchart: Fertilization
Sperm deposited in vagina
↓
Capacitation in female tract
Sperm gains fertilizing ability
↓
Sperm reaches ampulla of fallopian tube
↓
Acrosome reaction
Enzymes dissolve zona pellucida
↓
Sperm penetrates ovum
↓
Cortical reaction
Blocks polyspermy
↓
Pronuclei fusion (syngamy)
↓
Zygote formed (2n) → Cleavage begins
16:
Flowchart: Fetal Circulation
Oxygenated blood from placenta
via umbilical vein
↓
Ductus venosus
bypasses fetal liver → IVC
↓
Right atrium
↓
Foramen ovale
→ Left atrium → Left ventricle → Aorta
Right ventricle → Pulmonary artery
→ Ductus arteriosus → Aorta
↓
Systemic circulation (body tissues)
↓
Deoxygenated blood → umbilical arteries → placenta
17:
Flowchart: Functions of Placenta
Placenta
↓
Nutritive
Glucose, amino acids, fats to fetus
Respiratory
O₂ to fetus; CO₂ removed
Excretory
Urea, bilirubin removed
↓
Endocrine
HCG, HPL, oestrogen, progesterone
Protective
Partial barrier to pathogens
Immunological
IgG transfer → passive immunity
↓
Fetal growth and development ensured
19:
Flowchart: Oogenesis & Ovulation
Oogonium (fetal ovary)
↓
Primary oocyte
Arrested at Prophase I
↓
Primordial follicle → Primary follicle
FSH stimulation at puberty
↓
Secondary follicle
Antrum forms; oestrogen secreted
↓
Graafian (tertiary) follicle
LH surge triggers Meiosis I completion
↓
Ovulation — secondary oocyte released
↓
Not fertilized
Oocyte degenerates
Fertilized → Meiosis II completes → Ovum
↓
Corpus luteum forms → progesterone
↓
Corpus albicans (if no pregnancy)
21:
Flowchart: Fertilization & Implantation
Sperm + Secondary oocyte (ampulla)
↓
Fertilization → Zygote (2n)
↓
Cleavage divisions
Zygote travels along fallopian tube
↓
Morula (16 cells, day 3–4)
↓
Blastocyst (day 5–6)
Inner cell mass + trophoblast
↓
Zona pellucida hatches
↓
Implantation in endometrium (day 6–10)
Posterior wall of uterus, upper segment
22:
Flowchart: Signs & Symptoms of Pregnancy
Signs & Symptoms of Pregnancy
↓
Presumptive Signs
↓
Amenorrhoea, nausea, vomiting
↓
Breast changes, fatigue
↓
Urinary frequency, quickening
Probable Signs
↓
Uterine enlargement
↓
Hegar's, Goodell's, Chadwick's signs
↓
Ballottement, urine pregnancy test
Positive Signs
↓
Foetal heart sounds (Doppler/fetoscope)
↓
Foetal movements felt by examiner
↓
Ultrasound: gestational sac / foetus
↓
Pregnancy Confirmed
23:
Flowchart: Physiological Changes — 1st Trimester
First Trimester (0–13 Weeks)
↓
Reproductive System
↓
Uterus enlarges (pear → orange)
↓
Hegar's, Goodell's, Chadwick's signs
↓
Leucorrhoea, cervical softening
Cardiovascular
↓
Blood volume starts increasing
↓
Heart rate rises ~10 bpm
↓
BP slightly falls (vasodilation)
GI / Metabolic
↓
Nausea, vomiting (hCG peak)
↓
Increased appetite / food aversions
↓
Constipation (progesterone effect)
↓
Foetal Organogenesis Complete by 13 Weeks
24:
Flowchart: Physiological Changes — Antenatal Period
Physiological Changes in Pregnancy
↓
Cardiovascular
↓
Blood volume ↑ 40–50%
↓
Cardiac output ↑, BP ↓ mid-trimester
Respiratory
↓
Tidal volume ↑, RR unchanged
↓
Diaphragm elevated 4 cm
Renal
↓
GFR ↑ 50%, urinary frequency
↓
Glycosuria may occur normally
↓
Haematological
↓
Haemodilution (physiological anaemia)
↓
WBC rises, hypercoagulable state
Endocrine / Metabolic
↓
Insulin resistance ↑ (2nd/3rd trimester)
↓
BMR ↑ 15–20%
Musculoskeletal / Skin
↓
Lordosis, ligament laxity
↓
Striae, pigmentation changes
↓
Adaptations Support Foetal Growth & Labour
25:
Flowchart: Skin Changes During Pregnancy
Skin Changes in Pregnancy
↓
Pigmentation Changes
↓
Linea nigra (midline abdomen)
↓
Chloasma / melasma (face)
↓
Areola darkening (primary/secondary)
Stretch Marks
↓
Striae gravidarum (abdomen, breasts)
↓
Pink/purple → silver post-delivery
Vascular Changes
↓
Spider naevi (oestrogen effect)
↓
Palmar erythema
↓
Varicose veins, oedema
↓
Hormone-Driven; Most Resolve Postpartum
26:
Flowchart: Cardiovascular Changes in Pregnancy
Rising Oestrogen & Progesterone
↓
Peripheral vasodilation
↓
SVR falls → BP decreases (2nd trimester)
↓
Plasma volume ↑ 40–50% (by 32 wks)
↓
Stroke volume ↑, HR ↑ 10–15 bpm
↓
Cardiac output ↑ 30–50% (peaks 28–32 wks)
↓
Supine hypotension: IVC compression
Physiological anaemia (haemodilution)
↓
Returns to Baseline 6 Weeks Postpartum
27:
Flowchart: History Taking / Booking Visit
Patient Arrives for Booking Visit
↓
Identification: Name, age, address, registration
↓
Chief complaint & LMP / EDD calculation
↓
Obstetric history (G/P/A/L)
↓
Medical, surgical & family history
↓
General & obstetric physical examination
↓
Investigations: Hb, blood group, urine, USG
↓
Risk stratification (low / high risk)
↓
Individualised ANC Plan & Counselling
28:
Flowchart: Importance of Antenatal Care
Early Registration (<12 Weeks)
↓
Baseline assessment: history, examination, investigations
↓
Risk identification (anaemia, HTN, GDM, infections)
↓
Low Risk
↓
Routine ANC schedule (4–8 visits)
↓
Health education, supplements, immunisation
High Risk
↓
Enhanced monitoring & referral
↓
Specialist care, hospital delivery
↓
Monitoring: foetal growth, maternal well-being
↓
Safe Delivery & Healthy Mother & Baby
29:
Flowchart: Booking Visit — ANC Process
First ANC Contact (<12 Weeks Ideal)
↓
Registration & consent
↓
Complete history (obstetric, medical, family)
↓
General & obstetric examination
↓
Investigations: Hb, ABO/Rh, urine R/M, VDRL, HIV, USG
↓
Supplements: IFA, calcium, folic acid
↓
TT immunisation & birth preparedness
↓
Health education & diet counselling
↓
ANC Card Issued; Next Visit Scheduled
30:
Flowchart: ANC Schedule / Visits
Pregnancy Confirmed
↓
Visit 1 — Before 12 weeks (booking)
↓
Visit 2 — 14–16 weeks (anomaly screening)
↓
Visit 3 — 28 weeks (GDM screen, TT-2, growth)
↓
Visit 4 — 32 weeks (presentation, anaemia recheck)
↓
Visit 5 — 36 weeks (engagement, birth plan)
↓
Visit 6 — 40 weeks (post-dates assessment)
↓
Birth Preparedness & Safe Delivery Plan
32:
Flowchart: Minor Ailments of Pregnancy by Trimester
Minor Ailments of Pregnancy
↓
1st Trimester
↓
Nausea & vomiting (morning sickness)
↓
Urinary frequency, fatigue
↓
Breast tenderness, heartburn
2nd Trimester
↓
Constipation, flatulence
↓
Backache, round ligament pain
↓
Leucorrhoea, leg cramps
3rd Trimester
↓
Oedema, varicose veins
↓
Dyspnoea, insomnia
↓
Haemorrhoids, urinary frequency
↓
Managed with Education, Lifestyle & Symptomatic Relief
34:
Flowchart: Management of Nausea & Vomiting in Pregnancy
Nausea & Vomiting in Pregnancy
↓
Assess severity: mild / moderate / severe (hyperemesis)
↓
Mild–Moderate
↓
Small frequent meals; avoid triggers
↓
Ginger, dry crackers, cold fluids
↓
Pyridoxine (Vit B6) ± antiemetic
Hyperemesis Gravidarum
↓
Hospitalise; IV fluids (N/S or RL)
↓
IV antiemetics (ondansetron, metoclopramide)
↓
Correct electrolyte imbalance
↓
Thiamine supplementation
↓
Monitor Weight, Hydration & Foetal Well-Being
35:
Flowchart: Gestational Diabetes Mellitus Management
Screen All Pregnant Women at 24–28 Weeks
↓
GCT: 50 g glucose → plasma glucose at 1 hr
↓
GCT <140 mg/dL → Routine ANC
GCT ≥140 mg/dL → OGTT (75 g / 100 g)
↓
GDM Diagnosed (IADPSG / ADA criteria)
↓
Medical nutrition therapy + exercise (2 weeks)
↓
Targets met → Continue diet therapy
Targets not met → Insulin therapy
↓
Monitoring: FBS, PPBS, foetal growth scans
↓
Delivery plan: 38–39 weeks (or earlier if indicated)
↓
Postpartum OGTT at 6 weeks
36:
Flowchart: Pre-eclampsia Management
BP ≥140/90 mmHg after 20 Weeks + Proteinuria
↓
Mild Pre-eclampsia
↓
Hospitalise; bed rest (left lateral)
↓
Antihypertensives (methyldopa / labetalol)
↓
Foetal monitoring; daily urine protein
↓
Deliver at 37 weeks
Severe Pre-eclampsia
↓
ICU / HDU admission
↓
IV labetalol / hydralazine / nifedipine
↓
MgSO₄ (seizure prophylaxis)
↓
Deliver at 34 weeks (or immediately)
↓
Eclampsia: ABC + MgSO₄ + Emergency Delivery
37:
Flowchart: Nutritional Assessment / MUAC
Nutritional Assessment in Pregnancy
↓
MUAC measurement (non-dominant arm)
↓
MUAC ≥23 cm
↓
Normal nutritional status
↓
Routine diet counselling
MUAC 21–22.9 cm
↓
Moderate undernutrition
↓
Dietary supplementation + monitoring
MUAC <21 cm
↓
Severe acute malnutrition
↓
Refer; therapeutic nutrition support
↓
Reassess Monthly; Optimise Foetal Growth
38:
Flowchart: Iron Deficiency Anaemia in Pregnancy
Screen Hb at Booking, 28 & 36 Weeks
↓
Hb ≥11 g/dL → Prophylactic IFA daily
Hb 7–10.9 g/dL → Mild/Moderate anaemia
Hb <7 g/dL → Severe anaemia
↓
Mild–Moderate
↓
Oral ferrous sulphate 200 mg TDS
↓
Folic acid + Vit C; diet counselling
↓
Recheck Hb after 4 weeks
Severe
↓
Hospitalise; IV iron sucrose
↓
Blood transfusion if Hb <6 g/dL
↓
Managed delivery in hospital
↓
Target Hb ≥11 g/dL Before Delivery
56:
Flowchart: Uterotonic Drugs in Third Stage
Uterotonic / Oxytocic Drugs
↓
Oxytocin
↓
10 IU IM / IV infusion
↓
Stimulates uterine contraction
Ergometrine
↓
0.2 mg IM / IV
↓
Sustained tonic contraction
Misoprostol
↓
600 mcg oral / sublingual
↓
Prostaglandin E1 analogue
Carboprost
↓
250 mcg IM every 15 min
↓
Prostaglandin F2α analogue
↓
Prevention & Treatment of PPH
57:
Flowchart: Retained Placenta Management
Placenta not delivered >30 min
↓
IV access + IV oxytocin infusion
↓
Assess: bladder empty, cord intact
↓
Controlled cord traction (CCT)
↓
Placenta delivered?
↓
Yes → Monitor & complete care
No → Manual removal under anaesthesia
↓
IV antibiotics post-procedure
↓
Monitor for PPH & infection
58:
Flowchart: Perineal Repair Procedure
Assess perineal tear / episiotomy
↓
Classify degree (1st–4th)
↓
Administer local anaesthesia
↓
Repair vaginal mucosa (continuous suture)
↓
Repair perineal muscle layer
↓
Repair perineal skin (subcuticular)
↓
Rectal examination to confirm integrity
↓
Post-repair: analgesia & hygiene care
59:
Flowchart: Shoulder Dystocia Management
Head delivered — body not following
↓
Call for help immediately
↓
McRoberts manoeuvre (hyperflexion of thighs)
↓
Suprapubic pressure (Rubin I)
↓
Delivery of posterior arm
↓
Internal rotational manoeuvres (Rubin II / Woods screw)
↓
Last resort: deliberate clavicle fracture / Zavanelli
↓
Deliver baby — neonatal resuscitation ready
60:
Flowchart: Uterine Rupture Management
Suspect uterine rupture
↓
Sudden severe abdominal pain + shock
↓
Call senior obstetrician + team
↓
2 large-bore IV lines + fluid resuscitation
↓
Cross-match blood + consent for surgery
↓
Emergency laparotomy
↓
Repair rupture (uterine conservation)
Hysterectomy (uncontrolled bleeding)
↓
ICU monitoring + antibiotics post-op
62:
Flowchart: Involution of Uterus
Immediately Postpartum
1000g, at umbilicus
↓
Day 1: 1 cm above umbilicus
Uterus rises briefly
↓
Day 3: 3 cm below umbilicus
~750g, lochia rubra
↓
Day 7: Midway symphysis-umbilicus
~500g, lochia serosa
↓
Day 14: Behind pubic symphysis
~350g, lochia alba
↓
Week 6: Pre-pregnant size
~60g, involution complete
64:
Flowchart: Postnatal Exercises Progression
Day 1–2: Breathing & foot exercises
Deep breathing, ankle circles
↓
Day 3–7: Pelvic floor exercises
Kegel, abdominal tightening
↓
Week 2: Gentle abdominal exercises
Head lifts, leg slides
↓
Week 4: Pelvic tilts & bridges
Core strengthening begins
↓
Week 6: Full exercise resumption
Cleared at postnatal check
66:
Flowchart: Management of Puerperal Pyrexia
Temp >38°C after 24 hrs postpartum
↓
Identify cause
UTI, mastitis, wound, DVT, endometritis
↓
Investigations
CBC, urine/wound/blood culture, USG
↓
Medical treatment
Appropriate antibiotics, antipyretics
↓
Nursing care
Hydration, hygiene, vitals monitoring
↓
Monitor & prevent complications
Septicaemia, peritonitis, DVT
67:
Flowchart: Management of Mastitis & Breast Abscess
Early mastitis: pain, redness, fever
↓
Continue breastfeeding / expression
Empty breast, warm compresses
↓
Antibiotics (Cloxacillin/Flucloxacillin)
Analgesics, rest, hydration
↓
Resolves
Continue feeding, complete antibiotics
Abscess forms
Fluctuant swelling, high fever
↓
Surgical incision & drainage
Stop feeding affected side
↓
Prevention
Correct latch, nipple care, hygiene
68:
Flowchart: Breastfeeding Technique
Comfortable maternal positioning
Cradle / cross-cradle / football hold
↓
Correct infant attachment
Wide mouth, areola covered, chin touches breast
↓
Rooting & suck-swallow reflex
Baby actively feeding
↓
Let-down reflex triggered
Oxytocin release, milk ejection
↓
Signs of adequate feeding
8–12 feeds/day, wet nappies, weight gain
↓
Burping & safe positioning after feed
69:
Flowchart: Management of Postpartum Depression
Screen all mothers with EPDS
Score >10 = at risk
↓
Postnatal Blues (Day 3–5)
Reassurance, support, watchful waiting
Mild–Moderate PPD
Counselling, CBT, social support
Severe PPD
Antidepressants (SSRIs) + psychiatry referral
↓
Nursing care
Empathy, education, involve partner/family
↓
Follow-up & relapse prevention
71:
Flowchart: Immediate Care of Newborn at Birth
Baby Delivered
↓
Dry & Warm (radiant warmer)
↓
Clear Airway (suction mouth, nose)
↓
Assess Breathing & APGAR Score
↓
Clamp & Cut Umbilical Cord
↓
Skin-to-Skin & Eye Care (erythromycin)

Vitamin K 1 mg IM
↓
Initiate Breastfeeding (within 1 hr)

↓

↓
Eyes, Ears, Nose, Mouth (EENT)
↓
Neck & Clavicles (fracture check)

73:
Flowchart: APGAR Score & Action
Assess at 1 & 5 Minutes
↓
Score 5 Signs (0–2 each): HR, RR, Tone, Reflex, Colour
↓
Score 0–3 (Severe Depression)
Score 4–6 (Moderate Depression)
Score 7–10 (Normal)
↓
Immediate Resuscitation (PPV, Intubation)
Stimulate, O₂, Reassess at 5 min
Routine Newborn Care
↓
Document Score & Outcome
74:
Flowchart: WHO 10 Steps to Successful Breastfeeding
Step 1: Written BF Policy in Facility
↓
Step 2: Train All Healthcare Staff
↓
Step 3: Inform Pregnant Women of BF Benefits
↓
Step 4: Skin-to-Skin & BF within 1 Hour
↓
Step 5: Show Mothers How to Breastfeed
↓
Step 6: Give Only Breast Milk (no supplements)
↓
Step 7: Rooming-In 24 Hours/Day
↓
Step 8: Breastfeed on Demand
↓
Step 9: No Artificial Teats or Pacifiers

75:
Flowchart: Neonatal Sepsis & Cord Care Management
Suspect Sepsis (Lethargy, Poor Feed, Fever/Hypothermia)
↓
Investigations: CBC, CRP, Blood Culture
↓
Early-Onset Sepsis (<72 hrs)
Late-Onset Sepsis (>72 hrs)
↓
IV Antibiotics (Ampicillin + Gentamicin)
↓
Supportive Care: IV Fluids, O₂, Warmth
↓
Cord Care: Dry Method, Clean & Dry (no spirit)
↓
Fold Nappy Below Cord Stump
↓
Monitor & Cord Falls Off in 7–10 Days
76:
Flowchart: Neonatal Jaundice Management
Jaundice Detected in Neonate
↓
Assess Onset, Duration & Serum Bilirubin
↓
Physiological (Day 2–3, resolves <2 wks)
Pathological (Day 1, prolonged/severe)
↓
Frequent Breastfeeding & Monitor
Investigate Cause (Rh/ABO, Infection)
↓
Phototherapy (Bilirubin above threshold)
↓
Exchange Transfusion (if critically high)
↓
Monitor Bilirubin & Prevent Kernicterus
77:
Flowchart: Developmental Milestones by Age

↓

↓

↓

↓
Sources
Fetal urine (main), lung fluid, amnion, fetal skin (early)
Functions
Cushions fetus, maintains temp, permits movement, prevents adhesions
Normal Volume
500–1000 mL at term
↓
Assessment Methods
↓
AFI (Amniotic Fluid Index)
Normal 8–18 cm
Single Deepest Pocket
Normal 2–8 cm
Amniocentesis
Cytology, biochemistry
↓
Polyhydramnios
AFI >18 cm; GDM, fetal anomalies
Oligohydramnios
AFI <5 cm; renal agenesis, IUGR
91:
Flowchart: Estimation of Gestational Age
Estimate Gestational Age
↓
LMP (Naegele's Rule)
↓
LMP + 9 months + 7 days = EDD
↓
Reliable if regular cycles & known LMP
Ultrasonography
↓
1st trimester: CRL (most accurate)
↓
2nd trimester: BPD / FL / AC
Fundal Height
↓
Symphysis → fundus (cm ≈ weeks ±2)
↓
Reliable 20–36 weeks
Ballard Score (Neonate)
↓
Neuromuscular + physical maturity criteria
↓
Post-delivery gestational age estimation
↓
Confirmed EDD & Delivery Planning
92:
Flowchart: Fundal Height Measurement
Patient in Dorsal Recumbent Position
↓
Empty bladder confirmed
↓
Locate symphysis pubis (lower fixed point)
↓
Palpate uterine fundus (upper point)
↓
Measure with tape (cm) symphysis → fundus
↓
cm ≈ weeks of gestation (±2 cm)
↓
Small for dates → IUGR / oligohydramnios?
Large for dates → Twins / polyhydramnios?
↓
Document on Growth Chart; Refer if Abnormal
95:
Flowchart: Leucorrhoea in Pregnancy — Management
Vaginal Discharge in Pregnancy
↓
Assess: colour, odour, consistency, pruritus
↓
Physiological Leucorrhoea
↓
White, non-offensive, mild
↓
Reassurance + hygiene education
↓
Cotton underwear; avoid douching
Pathological (Infection)
↓
Swab culture: BV / Candida / Trichomonas
↓
BV → metronidazole; Candida → clotrimazole
↓
Treat partner if STI suspected
↓
Resolve Infection; Monitor for Preterm Labour Risk
96:
Flowchart: Varicose Veins in Pregnancy — Management
Varicose Veins Identified in Pregnancy
↓
Assess site: legs / vulva / haemorrhoids
↓
Non-pharmacological measures
↓
Elevate legs when resting
Elastic compression stockings
Avoid prolonged standing
↓
Avoid tight clothing; exercise (walking)
↓
Cold compresses for vulval varicosities
↓
Monitor for thrombophlebitis / DVT signs
↓
Most Resolve Spontaneously Postpartum
97:
Flowchart: Constipation in Pregnancy — Management
Constipation Reported in Pregnancy
↓
Causes: progesterone → gut motility ↓; iron supplements
↓
Dietary measures
↓
High-fibre diet (fruits, vegetables, bran)
Adequate fluid intake (2–3 L/day)
Regular light exercise (walking)
↓
Regular bowel habits; respond to urge promptly
↓
If refractory → ispaghula husk (safe laxative)
↓
Avoid stimulant laxatives in pregnancy
↓
Relief Achieved; Prevent Haemorrhoids
98:
Flowchart: Induction of Labour Methods
Decision to induce labour
↓
Assess Bishop score
↓
Unfavourable cervix → Prostaglandins (PGE2 gel / Misoprostol)
Favourable cervix → Proceed to ARM
↓
Artificial rupture of membranes (ARM)
↓
Oxytocin IV infusion (titrated)
↓
Continuous fetal monitoring (CTG)
↓
Vaginal delivery or LSCS if failed
99:
Flowchart: Caesarean Section Nursing Care
Pre-operative Care
↓
Informed consent + investigations
↓
NPO, IV line, catheterisation, shave prep
↓
Anaesthesia (spinal / general)
↓
Intra-operative Care
↓
Monitor vitals + fetal heart rate
↓
Assist surgeon + neonatal team ready
↓
Post-operative Care
↓
Vitals, uterine tone, wound, catheter output
↓
Analgesia, ambulation, breastfeeding support
↓
Discharge with wound care instructions
100:
Flowchart: Forceps Delivery Process
Indication confirmed for forceps
↓
Check prerequisites (fully dilated, engaged, membranes ruptured)
↓
Adequate analgesia / pudendal block
↓
Episiotomy performed
↓
Apply forceps blades (left then right)
↓
Traction with contractions (Pajot's manoeuvre)
↓
Deliver head, remove forceps
↓
Check for perineal / cervical trauma
101:
Flowchart: Vacuum Extraction Process
Indication confirmed for ventouse
↓
Prerequisites met (fully dilated, engaged head)
↓
Select cup size; apply to flexion point
↓
Create negative pressure (0.2 → 0.8 kg/cm²)
↓
Traction with contractions + maternal pushing
↓
Maximum 3 pulls / 3 detachments rule
↓
Release suction; deliver head
↓
Assess neonate for cephalhaematoma / trauma
102:
Flowchart: Bishop Score Classification
Assess Cervix (Bishop Score)
↓
Parameters: Dilation, Effacement, Station, Consistency, Position
↓
Score 0–4: Unfavourable cervix
↓
Cervical ripening required (PGE2 / Misoprostol)
Score 5–8: Intermediate cervix
↓
ARM ± Oxytocin infusion
Score >8: Favourable cervix
↓
ARM alone usually sufficient
↓
Proceed with induction / monitor progress
103:
Flowchart: Management of Puerperal Psychosis
Acute onset within 2 weeks postpartum
Delusions, hallucinations, confusion
↓
Immediate psychiatric assessment
Risk to mother and baby
↓
Hospitalisation (Mother & Baby Unit)
Safe environment, 24-hr observation
↓
Pharmacotherapy
Antipsychotics ± mood stabilisers ± antidepressants
↓
Nursing care
Safety, hygiene, nutrition, infant bonding support
↓
Family education & psychotherapy
↓
Discharge planning & long-term follow-up
104:
Flowchart: Management of Secondary PPH
Secondary PPH: bleeding 24 hrs–6 wks postpartum
↓
Identify cause
RPOC, endometritis, coagulation defect
↓
Investigations
CBC, coagulation profile, USG pelvis, HVS
↓
Resuscitation
IV access, fluids, blood transfusion if needed
↓
Infection present
Broad-spectrum antibiotics
RPOC on USG
Surgical evacuation (ERPC)
↓
Monitor vitals, lochia & haemoglobin
105:
Flowchart: Management of Retained Products of Conception
Suspect RPOC
Subinvolution, PPH, offensive lochia, fever
↓
Confirm diagnosis
USG pelvis: echogenic tissue in uterine cavity
↓
Stabilise patient
IV fluids, blood transfusion, antibiotics
↓
Surgical evacuation (ERPC)
Under GA; suction/curettage of cavity
↓
Send specimen for histopathology
↓
Post-op care & follow-up USG
106:
Flowchart: Perineal Care Process
Assess perineum after delivery
Degree of tear / episiotomy
↓
Clean with warm sterile water
Front-to-back technique
↓
Inspect suture line each shift
Redness, oedema, discharge, dehiscence
↓
Pain management
Ice packs, analgesics, sitz bath
↓
Hygiene education
Pad change every 4 hrs, hand hygiene
↓
Healing confirmed & discharge teaching
107:
Flowchart: Diagnosis & Management of Subinvolution
Suspect subinvolution
Uterus large, soft, lochia persists
↓
Identify cause
RPOC, endometritis, fibroids, full bladder
↓
Investigations
USG pelvis, HVS, CBC, urine culture
↓
Infection
Antibiotics (broad-spectrum)
RPOC
Ergometrine + ERPC
No clear cause
Oxytocics / ergometrine course
↓
Encourage breastfeeding
Stimulates oxytocin, aids involution
↓
Reassess uterine size at follow-up
